title: install.ps1: PS 5.1 parse fix (ASCII-only) + same flag changes + encoding guard test
action: Replace every non-ASCII character in install.ps1 (six em-dashes on lines 1,6,42,147,149,166) with ASCII; apply the same flag/flow changes as ih-3 (-GlobalSkills, -NoClaudeMd, layer-1 global copy conditional). Add an encoding regression test (any platform, no pwsh): fail if any byte > 0x7F exists in scripts/*.ps1. Root cause documented in CONTEXT E2: UTF-8-no-BOM em-dash decodes as cp1252 smart quote in Windows PowerShell 5.1 and terminates strings.

truths: grep -P non-ASCII on scripts/install.ps1 finds nothing
Encoding guard test fails on a non-ASCII byte planted in a .ps1 and passes on the real file
powershell.exe Parser::ParseFile reports zero errors on this machine

trace.outcome: install.ps1 made ASCII-only (6 em-dashes replaced, PS 5.1 parses clean via Parser::ParseFile) and given flag/flow parity with D1/D3: -GlobalSkills (opt-in global copy, was unconditional), -NoClaudeMd (--no-claude-md pass-through), -ClaudeMd kept as compat no-op; header/examples updated for per-project default layout. Added an encoding regression guard test (pure node fs, any platform) to test_onboard_bee.mjs that scans every scripts/*.ps1 for bytes > 0x7F and self-tests the detector against planted scratch files.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": ["skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs full suite (sections 1-11, sticky repo-hooks, skill-sync D1-D5 cases)"],
  "tests_added_or_changed": ["skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs new section 12 'encoding regression guard': asserts scripts/*.ps1 files are discovered (non-vacuous), asserts zero bytes > 0x7F across all of them, and self-tests the byte-scan detector against a scratch .ps1 (outside the repo, in os.tmpdir()) with a planted em-dash (must flag) and a clean ASCII scratch file (must pass)"],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Pre-change scripts/install.ps1 (git show HEAD:scripts/install.ps1) contained 6 em-dashes (lines 1,6,42,147,149,166) and was UTF-8 without BOM. Running the Windows PowerShell 5.1 Parser::ParseFile check against that pre-change content (via WSL interop, powershell.exe) produced a cascade of real parse errors (e.g. 'line 141: Missing closing )', 'line 39: Missing closing }', 'line 148: Unexpected token', 'line 190: string is missing the terminator'), matching CONTEXT.md E2's root-cause analysis (cp1252 decode turns the em-dash's trailing 0x94 byte into a smart quote that terminates a string mid-line). Also, pre-change layer-1 always copied skills into ~/.claude/skills and ~/.codex/skills unconditionally (no -GlobalSkills gate) and had no -NoClaudeMd/-GlobalSkills pass-through to onboard_bee.mjs, out of parity with the D1/D3 locked decisions already implemented in onboard_bee.mjs.",
  "verification_run": "grep -nP '[^\\x00-\\x7F]' scripts/install.ps1 -> empty (0 matches). node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs -> PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 1 (pre-existing case-alias skip, unrelated). WSL interop powershell.exe [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile against the POST-change scripts/install.ps1 -> 'PARSE OK' (zero errors), versus the pre-change run above which produced a real error cascade.",
  "deliberate_exceptions": []
}
